events.title: Wasatch Half Marathon and 10K
events.description: Come join us in the cool mountain scenery at The Wasatch Half Marathon and 10k! We are really to share this wonderful mostly trail run course to the race calendar. This event is one you don't want to miss. Starting and ending near the Soldier Hollow Nordic Center they’ll be no shortage of breathtaking scenery with the rewards of accomplishing something great!  No need for shuttles at this event as you’ll be able to park and the start/finish line.
events.location: Midway, UT

events.title: Needle Felting Class with Plum Diddle
events.description: Spend a summer evening learning the sweetest craft with Plum Diddle! This event includes supplies and instruction for three needle felted fruit designs . Guests will create at their own pace during class and take home their supplies to keep crafting after the event. No experience needed. Come ready for a fun, creative night! Only 15 spots Available! * Due to material costs and teacher preparati...
events.location: The Dainty Pear Co., 152 W 100 N, Midway, UT 84049

events.title: Heber Market on Main
events.description: Over the past two decades, the Heber City Farmer’s Market has been a weekly, summertime event that has welcomed visitors and locals alike to gather together in downtown Heber City to enjoy local food, drink, shopping, and live music. SCHEDULE: 5:00 - 9:00 PM: Market with booths and vendors 6:30 - 8:30 PM: Live music and FREE to all Public parking surrounds the park block except for no parking on 100 West. Additional parking at the Public Safety Building. hebermarket.com/events
events.location: Heber City Main Street, Heber City, UT 84032

events.title: Wasatch BoomerFest
events.description: Join us for Wasatch Boomerfest, an incredible 3-day music festival in August at the Richard W. Erickson Foundation located in beautiful Wallsburg, Utah. There will be vendor booths, food trucks, activities for the kids, and several well-known bands performing each day. You can purchase a single day ticket or get the weekend pass to make sure you don't miss any of the fun. Onsite camping also available!
events.location: Richard W Erickson Foundation Antique Power Museum, R. W. Erickson Foundation 00-0010-2991, Wallsburg, UT
events.address: R. W. Erickson Foundation 00-0010-2991, Wallsburg, UT
events.venue_name: Richard W Erickson Foundation Antique Power Museum

events.title: Midway Farmers Market
events.description: The market is held every Saturday from 10:00 am to 1:00 pm at the Midway Town Square from June through October. Fresh local produce and local artisans are onsite selling goods at the farmer's market in Midway. See you there!
events.location: Midway Town Square, 200 W Main Street Midway, Utah 84049

events.title: Charleston July Pioneer Day
events.description: Come out and celebrate Pioneer Day in the quaint town of Charleston! The day starts at 6:45 am with a flag-raising ceremony, 5K race begins at 7:00 am, followed by a hearty pancake breakfast, vendor booths, car show, family-friendly activities, evening live entertainment, and nighttime stunning fireworks display. Schedule of Events: 6:00 AM Wake Up Call 6:45 AM Flag Raising 7:00 - 9:00 AM Charleston Town Breakfast 7:00 AM Charleston 5K - Join us for a community 5K run or walk open to all ages and fitness levels. 8:00 AM Fun Run (kids 12 and under) 8:00 - 10:30 AM Cornhole Tournament Sign-ups 9:00 AM - 1:00 PM Car Show 10:00 AM - 2:00 PM Vendors & Food Trucks 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM - Silent Auction 10:00 AM - Local Entertainment 11:00 AM Annual Cornhole Tournament 11:00 PM Kids Games 1:00 PM Root Beer Chug 1:30 PM Splash & GRAB 2:30 3:00 PM Live Auction 3:00 PM - (weather permitting) Tethered Hot Air Balloon Rides 6:00 PM Parade - parade route starts at Soldier Hollow Charter School and ends at Charleston Park 7:00 PM - Headliner entertainment All Day - Music & Entertainment, Entertainment Stage Fireworks at Dark
events.location: Charleston Park, Charleston, Utah 84032

events.title: Midway Swiss Days
events.description: The annual Swiss Days Festival takes place in Midway, Utah, the Friday and Saturday before Labor Day each year and draws a huge crowd both days. Swiss Days includes a 10K race, parade, live entertainment, and so much more. DATES: Friday, September 4 & Saturday, September 5, 2026 SCHEDULE: Friday, Sep 4th: Market 8:00 am - 8:00 pm Saturday, Sep 5th: Market 8:00 am - 8:00 pm Breakfast & 10K Run start at 7:00 am, Parade at 10:00 am PARKING: Two public parking locations offer a free shuttle bus to and from the event location. Parking is $10 and the shuttle runs approximately every 15 minutes. There is limited street parking and private pay parking lots surrounding the square.‍ Complimentary bike parking is available at Centennial Park. PETS: Pets are NOT allowed at the venue.‍
events.location: Midway Town Square, 200 W Main Street Midway, Utah 84049

events.title: Fiddlers and Fireworks Train Ride
events.description: Celebrate Utah's Pioneer Day on our annual evening train - celebrated on Friday, July 24th. We'll start the fun at 7pm with live music and a fried chicken dinner, served outside next to the depot (7:15-8 pm). Dinner includes two pieces of fried chicken, potato salad, roll, a cookie and soft drinks/ bottled water. At 8:15 you'll enjoy the Old West gunfighter show, and then the 2 hour round trip departs at 8:30 pm. We travel to Decker Bay on the shores of Deer Creek Reservoir. After the run around at Decker Bay the train will stop on the shores of Deer Creek Reservoir directly across the lake from the Charleston Fireworks. We will enjoy the fireworks across the lake from 10 pm until about 10:15 and then re-board the train and travel back to the depot. This is a fun event for the whole family! Your ticket includes dinner at the Depot, live music during dinner, and a ride on the Historic Heber Train to watch the Charleston fireworks across the lake (Deer Creek Reservoir).
events.location: Heber Valley Railroad, 450 S 6th W, Heber City, UT 84032

events.title: Skate Competition
events.description: Watch or participate in the Wasatch County Fair Days Live Free, Ride Hard Skate Competition at Southfield Skate Park on July 30th from 5:00-8:00PM. There will be games, food, activities, and prizes! You can sign up to compete in the Skateboard, Scooter, or Bike (BMX) competitions by filling out the form at the button below.
events.location: Southfield Park, 895 W 100 S, Heber City, UT 84032

events.title: Midway Music on the Square
events.description: Midway's summer concert series is happening at Midway Town Square on Tuesday nights from 6 - 8 pm. Bring a blanket or lawn chair. There will be food trucks and great music for the whole family. Come and support local musicians. FREE to the public.
events.location: Midway Town Square, 200 W Main Street Midway, Utah 84049

events.title: Wasatch Back Art Festival
events.description: The second annual Wasatch Back Art Festival will be showcasing local artists, makers, and crafters. This two-day festival will be held at Midway (July 3-5) and Heber City (July 25-27), and seeks to support all artists in selling their original diverse artworks. All work must be the artist’s original creation. Plan to be at this art festival to see original local artwork!
events.location: Main Street Park Heber City, Main Street Park, 200 S Main St., Heber City UT
events.address: Main Street Park, 200 S Main St., Heber City UT

events.title: Park City Sailing Fall Regatta
events.description: Park City Sailing Association's Annual Fall Regatta is open to all sailors! We encourage all that register to be experienced sailors and know how to race. You do not need to be a member of Park City Sailing to race. Limited charters are available. Spectating is free with park entrance. Volunteer opportunities are available. See www.sailpc.org for details!
events.location: Jordanelle State Park, 515 UT-319, Heber City, UT 84032

events.title: Wasatch Coin %26 History Club Meeting
events.description: Wasatch Coin & History Club meets on the last Tuesday of each month (except December) at 6 PM in the Wasatch County Public Library, Heber City. FOR MORE INFO: Mark Avery @ 615-482-9747, averyancients.com popsavery1@gmail.com & Dr. Lee McKenzie - leefmck@gmail.com
events.location: Wasatch County Library, 465 E 1200 S, Heber City

events.title: Free Yoga on the Lawn @ Heber City Park
events.description: Enjoy this peaceful, guided yoga practice in a beautiful natural setting in the heart of Heber City. Bring your own yoga mat, water, and anything to help you feel comfortable. All yoga sessions are weather dependent. Sessions are all free, but donations are welcome.
events.location: Main Street Park Heber City, Main Street Park, 200 S Main St., Heber City UT
events.address: Main Street Park, 200 S Main St., Heber City UT

events.title: Soulful Sundays @ Heber City Park
events.description: Join us for a serene summer evening as Soulful Sundays shares local musical talents. This enchanting hour of music will fill the Heber City Main Street Park with soothing sounds, peaceful harmonies, and community enrichment. Every Sunday will feature new artists. Bring a blanket, gather with friends and family, and experience the magic of live music in the heart of Heber City. Admission is free—all are welcome!
events.location: Main Street Park Heber City, Main Street Park, 200 S Main St., Heber City UT
events.address: Main Street Park, 200 S Main St., Heber City UT

events.title: Wizards Train
events.description: Put on your robes and grab your wand and come and ride the Wizard's Train. It departs at 7 pm and includes a 90 minute round trip. Costumed staff of popular characters will entertain you on board. Your ride includes trivia, fun, great photo opportunities, and other surprises! Additional food and snacks available for purchase from the concession car. You can also book a Wizard's Train ticket for the Luxury First Class Car. All passengers (who want to) will get sorted on board with the Sorting Hat. We will serve you a delicious chocolate frog, and if you're brave enough, have you try a Bertie Botts Everyflavor Beans. There will be fun, and trivia on board. Don't miss this fun experience for the whole family. NOTE: This train has assigned seats. If you want to sit by another group who bought tickets separately, you must call us at least 24 hours before departure and let us know. We'll do our best to seat you together.
events.location: Heber Valley Railroad, 450 S 6th W, Heber City, UT 84032

events.title: Planes %26 Horsepower Car Show
events.description: Planes and Horsepower is back for its 11th year! The Utah Wing of the Commemorative Air Force is pleased to host one of Utah's most unique car shows. Patrons can bring their vehicles to get a unique photo op with very rare WWII aircraft. You will have as a souvenir from the show digital files of your photo op and physical prints as an optional bonus. Pre-registering for the show is strongly recommended, as we often fill up our available space in the airport about a week before the show. Proceeds from the show benefit the CAF UT-Wing aviation museum and assist us in our mission of preserving these historic aircraft in airworthy condition.
events.location: Commemorative Air Force UT Wing WWII Aviation Museum, 2365 Airport Road Heber City, Utah 84032
events.venue_name: Commemorative Air Force UT Wing WWII Aviation Museum

events.title: Wasatch County Library Community Concert Series
events.description: Free Concert Series @ Wasatch County Library The Wasatch County Library invites you to an evening of live music. The concert is family-friendly, free to attend, and open to everyone—bring a friend and join us at the library amphitheater during the summer months or indoors during winter and inclement weather for a vibrant night of music and community!
events.location: Wasatch County Library, 465 E 1200 S, Heber City

events.title: Movie Night @ Rock Cliff Nature Center
events.description: Join us every Thursday in January to watch some of the best nature documentaries to come out of Sundance! All movies start at 6:30 pm. Jan 8: dirt!: The Movie Jan 15: March of the Penguins Jan 22: Fire of Love Jan 29: Earth Days
events.location: Rock Cliff Nature Center, 2500 State Route 32 Kamas, Utah 84032

events.title: Dominick Antonelli @ Cafe Galleria
events.description: Live Summer Music Series May through September (weather permitting) Every day but Mondays from 5:30pm – 8:30pm Sundays 10am – 1:30pm Dominick has been playing guitar for 21 years and writing original music for 15+ of those years. He looks at himself not as a guitar player, but an artist who utilizes the guitar and other forms to create an inspiring, healing, and contemplative experience for himself and listeners. Alternate tunings and intuitive playing have contributed immensely by shaping the instrumental and lyrical music that comes through him. He has a background in Integrative Health and is currently studying Music and Recording Arts Technology which is taking his creative possibilities to new depths and heights. He has performed at festivals in Thailand, Costa Rica, and the East Coast (US), as well as in yoga classes, local gigs, and workshops where he incorporates live music, meditation, group energy healing and writing into one experience. He loves performing live and sharing the gifts that continue to be cultivated. Read more about Dominick
events.location: Cafe Galleria, 101 West Main Street

events.title: Saturday Sunset Music Series
events.description: There’s no better way to spend a summer evening in Heber City than at the Saturday Sunset Music Series. Every Saturday from 7:00–8:30 PM, Main Street Park comes alive with live music, good food, and a relaxed, family-friendly atmosphere as the sun sets over downtown. Saturday programming is part of the broader Music on Main season. The series features a diverse lineup of local musicians, highlighting the depth of talent in the Heber Valley.
events.location: Heber City Main Street, Heber City, UT 84032

events.title: Yoga in the Lavender Field @ Blue Canyon Lavender Farm
events.description: Enjoy a peaceful experience with outdoor yoga surrounded by the blooming lavender fields at Blue Canyon Lavender Farm in Heber City, Utah. Led by yoga instructor Marty Harger, this gentle one-hour practice combines fresh mountain air, the calming scent of lavender, and stunning views of Mount Timpanogos for a truly unique wellness experience. After yoga, guests will enjoy a guided tour of the farm, learn how lavender is grown, harvested, and distilled. Participants will also have time to explore the farm’s gift shop and receive a special lavender gift to take home. PRE-REGISTRATION IS REQUIRED for each yoga session.
events.location: Blue Canyon Farm, 1850 Edwards Lane Heber City, Utah 84032

events.title: Movies on the Lawn @ Grand Hyatt Deer Valley
events.description: We invite guests and locals to enjoy outdoor, decade-themed film screenings on Hailstone Terrace throughout the summer. Bring a blanket, purchase food and drinks, and relax under the stars with family-friendly favorites in a welcoming community atmosphere. Arrive early! Movies start at 8:30 PM. 2026 Movie Lineup June 19 — Finding Nemo (2000s Favorites) July 3 — Guardians of the Galaxy (2010s Hits) August 8 — The Super Mario Bros. Movie (2020s Family Films) September 4 — Back to the Future (Bonus Feature)
events.location: Grand Hyatt Deer Valley, 1702 Glencoe Mountain Wy, Park City, UT 84060
events.address: 1702 Glencoe Mountain Wy, Park City, UT 84060

events.title: WOW Clinic Summer Series Wellness Workshops
events.description: At The WOW Clinic, we believe feeling your best starts with understanding your body. This summer, we're bringing that mission to life with expert-led wellness workshops — each packed with practical tools, delicious samples, and a full hour of our sauna and cold plunge experience. Space is very limited. Don't miss your spot! Sign up using the QR Code or visit their website. June 10TH - THE PROTEIN BLUEPRINT: Learn exactly how much protein your body needs, taste delicious high-protein meals, and walk away with practical strategies to build strength, support metabolism, and feel full longer. June 24TH - TAMING INFLAMMATION THROUGH FOOD AND LIFESTYLE: Discover the hidden signs of chronic inflammation and explore the top diet and lifestyle shifts to reduce inflammation. Get our recommended lab list for uncovering hidden inflammation. July 8TH - MASTER YOUR BLOOD SUGAR: Ditch the energy crashes and sugar cravings--learn how to build blood-sugar-friendly meals, explore smarter sweetener options, and leave with recipes and strategies you can use immediately. July 22ND - SOLUTIONS FOR BLOATING, IBS, & DIGESTIVE DISTRESS: If gas, bloating, constipation, or unpredictable digestion are stealing your quality of life, this workshop will help you understand what's going on and give you real, actionable steps toward lasting relief.
events.location: WOW Clinic, 1716 North Highway 40, Suite 200 Heber City, Utah 84032
events.address: 1716 North Highway 40, Suite 200 Heber City, Utah 84032

events.title: Summer First Fridays @ Grand Hyatt Deer Valley
events.description: Kick off the summer weekends with First Fridays, a resort‑wide celebration running the first Friday of each month from June through August. Enjoy live music across the property and 25% off food at Remington Hall, Remington Lounge, Sunspell Pool, and Hidden Ace. From poolside beats to vinyl sets and evening performances, each Friday brings a full day of sound, flavor, and mountain‑town energy. Music Schedule: 2–5 PM — Sunspell Pool DJ set by the pool. Local guests may reserve day passes or daybeds through ResortPass. 6–8 PM — Remington Lounge Vinyl DJ spinning an eclectic evening mix. 8–11 PM — Hidden Ace Live music to close out the night in our speakeasy‑style venue.
events.location: Grand Hyatt Deer Valley, 1702 Glencoe Mountain Wy, Park City, UT 84060
events.address: 1702 Glencoe Mountain Wy, Park City, UT 84060

events.title: Labor Day BBQ
events.description: We're excited to celebrate Labor Day and the end of summer with a BBQ at the Lookout! On Monday, September 7th, we invite you to join us for delicious food prepared by our talented culinary team. Experience the mouthwatering fusion of smoky, char-grilled delights, set against the breathtaking views at the Lookout. We look forward to welcoming you to Sundance and sharing the joy and beauty of the mountains with you this Labor Day. Bring your friends, family, and your appetite! With each BBQ purchase, guests will receive a 50% discount on a scenic lift ride ticket. Soak in the panoramic vistas as you ride up the mountain, a perfect way to complement your meal. Guests will need to take their confirmation email or receipt of purchase of the Lookout BBQ to the ticket office to redeem the 50% off discount on scenic lift rides.
events.location: Sundance Resort, 8841 N. Alpine Loop Road Sundance, Utah 84604
events.address: 8841 N. Alpine Loop Road Sundance, Utah 84604

events.title: Wild West Taylor Western Weekend at Liberty Sanctuary
events.description: A Weekend Of Understanding Horses with Equine Clinician, West Taylor Liberty Sanctuary is thrilled to welcome back West & Kami Taylor for a weekend of workshops designed to help us understand the horse, how they think and behave, how that impacts development, and what horses teach us about ourselves. We will cap the weekend off with a native crafts workshop taught by West's wife and artist, Kami. Space is limited so please don't wait to sign up! West Taylor is a nationally recognized expert equine behaviorist with over 15 years of hands-on experience working with wild mustangs and troubled horses using science-based horsemanship. From his ranch at the base of Utah’s Fish Lake Mountains, West has dedicated thousands of hours to studying horse behavior at its purest form. His research focuses on the connection between a horse’s ability to relax and learn versus becoming stressed, anxious, or shut down. This deep understanding of the horse’s nervous system and emotional state forms the foundation of science-based horsemanship, a groundbreaking approach that can transform relationships between horses and humans -- and allows us to reflect on how this benefits our relationships with others in our daily lives. COME OUT FOR THE WEEKEND OR SELECT WHAT'S BEST FOR YOU! Welcome: Life Lessons Learned From Horses ~ Friday, July 17th 6-8 pm West Taylor's Science-Based Horsemanship Clinic (audit only) ~ Sat, July 18th 9-4:30 pm & Sun, July 19 9-12:00 Native Drum & Rattle Making with Kami Taylor ~ Sunday, 1 pm-5pm Known as a trusted problem-solver and often a “last hope” for dangerously reactive or traumatized horses, West brings a unique blend of science, empathy, and skill to his work. He has been featured in numerous films and documentaries about horses and mustangs, and he has coached countless horse owners on building safer, calmer, and more connected partnerships with their animals. [embed]https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=I_Kgm2tuQWs[/embed] [embed]https://www.youtube.com/w...
events.location: 585 Little Pole Rd, Heber City, UT 84032, USA, 585 Little Pole Rd, Heber City, UT 84032, USA

events.title: Cruisin’ + Burgers
events.description: Rev up your summer at Cruisin + Burgers —a fun-filled evening of cool cars, great food, and family-friendly entertainment! Hosted by Oldies Productions with J.C. Hackett in partnership with Shake Shack, this free community event takes place the second Tuesday of each month from 5–8 PM (weather permitting), May through September, at Junction Commons’ Entry Plaza near Shake Shack. Bring the whole family to check out an incredible lineup of vehicles of all years, makes, models, colors, and sizes, enjoy delicious bites from Shake Shack, and soak up the lively community atmosphere. Car owners can cruise in for a chance to win awards, dash plaques, prizes, Shake Shack gift cards, and more. 2026 Event Dates Tuesday, May 12 Tuesday, June 9 Tuesday, July 14 Tuesday, August 11 Tuesday, September 8 Free to attend and free to enter your vehicle!...
events.location: Junction Commons, North Landmark Drive, Park City, Utah

events.title: July Green Drinks
events.description: Join Recycle Utah to learn about Utah’s new Community Clean Energy Program and other renewable energy initiatives across the state. July Green Drinks will cover renewable energy initiatives in our communities and across the state. Join us for a bring-your-own food & drink picnic at Deer Meadows Ranch in Oakley! This event is free, but registration is required. This event is 21+. Please consider carpooling!
events.location: Deer Meadows Ranch, Oakley, UT

events.title: Tweens Who Read
events.description: Calling all readers ages 11–14! If you love great books and fun conversations, join the Tweens Who Read Book Club at Wasatch County Library. This month’s featured title is Chains by Laurie Halse Anderson. Participants receive a free copy of the book, courtesy of the Wasatch Community Foundation, then gather to enjoy snacks, discussion, and a hands-on activity inspired by the story. Sign up at Wasatch County Library. For questions, contact Brittnie at bhecht@wasatch.utah.gov. Visit library.wasatchcounty.gov to explore the full lineup of Wasatch County Library programs and events.
events.location: Wasatch County Library, 465 E 1200 S, Heber City

events.title: Bike ‘n’ Botany
events.description: Join us for a leisurely pedal to learn about common noxious weeds of the Snyderville Basin. We’ll start at the Swaner EcoCenter then ride the paved and packed gravel paths toward Willow Creek Park, stopping along the way to identify and discuss noxious weed species. Need to borrow a bike? Indicate this at registration and we’ll reach out with details about how to access a bike for free from Summit Bike Share for the ride.
events.location: Swaner Preserve and EcoCenter, 1258 Center Drive, Park City

events.title: Glide In Movies
events.description: Glide In Movies transforms our ice arena into a one‑of‑a‑kind summer hangout. Where guests can relax in the stands and enjoy a movie on the big screens or lace up and skate under the lights while the film plays. Admission to watch is completely free for the community. For those looking to make it even more memorable, guests are invited to bring their own skates or rent a pair on-site and take to the ice during the movie for a truly unique experience.
events.location: Location TBD

events.title: Park Silly Sunday Market
events.description: An ecofriendly open air market, street festival and commUnity forum where neighbors and friends come together to celebrate our community of fun and funky Park Sillians. With live music, vendors, gourmet foods and a beer garden and tiki bar ~ this is the place to be on select Sundays this summer on Historic Main Street in Park City.
events.location: Historic Main Street, Main Street, Park City, UT

events.title: July Listening Session
events.description: Explore the intersecting languages of quilting and synthesizers. On July 20, artists Mary Toscano and Andrew Rease Shaw lead a second Listening Session. Engage with their collaborative project, LFO, currently on view in (Re)sounding. Experience Toscano’s quilts and the corresponding compositions by Shaw. The artists will discuss their project while inviting reflections gained from an evening of close viewing and deep listening. RSVP at https://go.kimballartcenter.org/july-listening
events.location: Kimball Art Center, 1251 Kearns Blvd, Park City, UT

events.title: ART BAR – iPhone Photography Class with Amy Eskind
events.description: In one class you will learn the elements of a striking photograph and how to create one. Learn the basics of composing dramatic photos and become familiar with fun iPhone camera features and editing tools. We’ll discuss composition, editing tools, and more. *Bring your iPhone – this is a hands-on class. Amy Eskind is an award-winning iPhone photographer, as well as a Nikon devotee and owner of Images by Amy, LLC. Wine and light refreshments will be served.
events.location: Create PC, Kearns Boulevard, Park City, Utah

events.title: Wasatch Back Art Festival – Heber
events.description: The Wasatch Back Art Festival is a growing community art event held in Utah’s scenic Heber Valley, showcasing a wide range of original work from local artists and makers. The festival brings together painters, photographers, sculptors, and craftspeople for a vibrant outdoor experience celebrating creativity and craftsmanship. Visitors can explore artist booths, meet creators, and enjoy a relaxed, family-friendly atmosphere that highlights the unique culture and artistic talent of the Wasatch Back region. Heber Main Street Park (250 S. Main Street) July 24th 12pm – 6pm July 25th 10am – 6pn July 26th 10am – 6pm...

Learn the basics of composing dramatic photos and become familiar with fun iPhone camera features and editing tools. events.title: The Business Table
events.description: The Business Table is a monthly gathering hosted by Team Cheever Plumbing & HVAC intended to spark meaningful relationships among business owners in the Wasatch Back. This event is invite only. Please apply at the link below, and join us for great conversation and food at a local Park City restaurant! Sign up for our next event here!
events.location: Park City, UT

events.title: The Splinter Beneath a Fractured Star: Moses Williams & Chaz Prymek
events.description: Artists Moses Williams and Chaz Prymek collaborate on an immersive sound-and-video performance. The piece invites viewers to engage with space, time, and perception in new and unexpected ways. Join us July 31, 7:00 PM at the Kimball Art Center for this unique blending of original composition with moving image. RSVP for the Free event at go.kimballartcenter.org/Splinter-beneath
events.location: Kimball Art Center, 1251 Kearns Blvd, Park City, UT

events.title: Back 2 School Basics
events.description: Back 2 School Basics meets the needs of families in Summit and Wasatch counties by relieving some of the financial burdens of back-to-school shopping. Each participating student receives a gift card to spend at Junction Commons, a new backpack, and school supplies of their choosing. This program is supported by volunteers who give of their time, donors who sponsor children, and by grant funding. Back 2 School Basics serves roughly 1,600 students from Summit County, Wasatch County, and the Confederated Tribes of the Goshute Reservation in Ibapah, Utah. We are looking for dedicated donors, volunteers, and community support! Contact JaimeC@ccofpc.org or visit our website for more information....
events.location: Junction Commons, North Landmark Drive, Park City, Utah

events.title: Locals Night: Kimball Arts Festival
events.description: Come kick off the 57th Kimball Arts Festival! Friday night only, Summit County locals get in free! Come see nearly 200 artist booths representing 13 art mediums lining Main Street Park City, 2 stages featuring local musicians, food trucks, art activities, and more! Locals passes, day passes, and VIP pass upgrades (including skybox lounge access, food, exclusive programming, and a private bar) are available. Get tickets now at go.kimballartcenter.org/festival-tickets-2026
events.location: Kimball Art Center, 1251 Kearns Blvd, Park City, UT

events.title: Dominick Antonelli at Cafe Galleria
events.description: Dominick Antonelli is on his first national tour and he will be wrapping up his last official tour date in Midway, UT at Cafe Galleria on Saturday August 8th. He will be playing an evening of original music from 530-830pm. Please come out for an evening of story telling, great food and beverages, original music and to celebrate Dominick’s final stop on his first national tour. We look forward to seeing you!
events.location: Cafe Galleria, Midway, UT

events.title: Live PC Give PC
events.description: Live PC Give PC will take place on FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 6, 2026! HELP SHAPE PARK CITY’S FUTURE IN A SINGLE DAY. Every November, Live PC Give PC brings our community together through LivePCGivePC.org for 24 hours of extreme generosity. Children break open their piggy banks. Young people dip into their savings. Businesses kick in matching grants. With everyone in the community giving whatever they can, we’ve raised millions of dollars for local nonprofits. It’s how we’re working together to preserve the place we love—so it will continue to thrive for generations to come.
events.location: Park City, UT

events.title: 2-Day Throwing Large Forms on the Wheel
events.description: Learn how to throw large forms on the wheel in this two-day workshop (July 18-19) with Tom Caswell! Practice centering large amounts of clay, combining separate pieces to make one large form. This class is best suited for ceramic artists who are comfortable on the wheel and want to take their skills to the next level. Artwork will be fired by our dedicated clay studio staff. Remember to collect your masterpiece within 60 days to avoid recycling. Sign up at https://go.kimballartcenter.org/large-forms
events.location: Kimball Art Center, 1251 Kearns Blvd, Park City, UT
